intro: JavaScript excels at dynamic UI programming but can scale poorly across complex enterprise architectures. Transitioning to Kotlin provides a type-safe environment that runs seamlessly on the JVM, native binaries, or Android platforms. This shift helps development teams improve compile-time correctness, write clean code with structured concurrency, and scale backend services efficiently.

diffs.from: Dynamic type system with runtime type resolution.
diffs.to: Statically typed with smart type inference engines.

diffs.from: Allows null or undefined properties, causing runtime errors.
diffs.to: Strict compile-time separation of nullable and non-nullable types.

diffs.from: Single-thread event loop processing microtask steps.
Browser runtimes, Node.js, or specialized engine sandboxes.
diffs.to: JVM bytecode, Android system drivers, or compiled native binaries.

diffs.from: Standard object mappings or loose JSON schemas.

examples.title: Data Definition Layout
examples.description: Creating immutable data containers with automatic structural methods.
examples.toCode:
data class Employee(
    val id: Int,
    val email: String
)

examples.title: Null Safe Chains
examples.description: Invoking methods on fields that might be null without causing crashes.

tips.title: Leverage the Safe Call Operator
tips.body: Use the ?. operator along with the Elvis operator (?:) to handle potentially null values safely.

tips.title: Adopt Data Classes
tips.body: Replace basic JavaScript object models with Kotlin data classes to auto-generate toString, equals, and hashCode methods.

tips.title: Use Coroutines for Async Tasks
tips.body: Replace promises and async/await loops with Kotlin coroutines to run lightweight asynchronous tasks.

tips.title: Prefer val for Immutability
tips.body: Declare local variables using "val" to ensure immutability unless the value needs to be reassigned.
